title: CSS Basics Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential rules for styling your web content using Cascading Style Sheets.

questions.question: What is the primary role of 'CSS' (Cascading Style Sheets) in a web application?
options: To define the website's visual styling and layout (colors, fonts, etc.).
To provide a database that is only accessible during a specific time on the server.
To help users write HTML code using templates.
Both 'To define the website's visual styling' and 'To provide a database' are correct.

questions.question: Which of these is the MOST recommended way to apply styles to a professional website?
options: In a separate external .css file linked via the <link> tag.

questions.question: In a CSS rule (e.g., 'p { color: blue; }'), what is the 'p' part called?
In a CSS rule (e.g., 'p { color: blue; }'), what is the 'color: blue;' part called?
The 'Cascading' part of CSS means that the browser uses a specific set of rules (Importance, Specificity, Source Order) to decide which style ________?
options: Wins / is applied to an element.
Is translated into a different language.
